baseName: caps standalone vitest below parent-ledger grants

current.name: gives standalone Vitest the same ledger-governed cap as parent grants

rationale: The standalone cap assertion was deliberately strengthened for the no-queue scheduler: standalone Vitest now receives the same ledger-governed cap as an orchestrated parent rather than the obsolete single-process throttle. The replacement still asserts the base declaration's ledger cap boundary while pinning the updated invariant.

baseName: planLaneConcurrency never raises a lane above the per-process fork cap

current.name: fixes the unit cap at three and lets VITEST_MAX_WORKERS lower it only

rationale: The lane-specific per-process bound is now owned by the single Vitest process's fixed three-worker cap. The replacement exercises default, fractional, invalid, and above-cap requests to prove no environment value can raise that bound.

baseName: planLaneConcurrency keeps instantaneous forks (maxConcurrent*perLane) within the grant

current.name: fixes the unit cap at three and lets VITEST_MAX_WORKERS lower it only
keeps retry three across exactly four normal projects

rationale: There is no longer a grant multiplied across concurrent lane processes. The fixed-cap assertion and each normal project's resolved maxWorkers now preserve the original instantaneous-process bound directly in the four-project single-process model.

baseName: REGRESSION: the single-process throttle (grant=2) collapses lanes to serial

current.name: runs test:unit as one direct Vitest command with no lane or ledger import
fixes the unit cap at three and lets VITEST_MAX_WORKERS lower it only

rationale: The obsolete serial-lane failure mode is structurally removed: test:unit launches one Vitest process rather than feeding a ledger grant into a lane scheduler, while the replacement cap test pins three workers instead of the former two-worker throttle.

baseName: FIX: the fair orchestrator grant runs the 3 lanes concurrently

current.name: runs test:unit as one direct Vitest command with no lane or ledger import
keeps retry three across exactly four normal projects

rationale: The direct Vitest command now schedules the four explicit projects internally, replacing cross-process lane concurrency. These declarations pin both the single-process entrypoint and the complete normal project topology that retains parallel unit execution.

baseName: reserveVitestLaneBudget grants the FAIR core budget, not the single-process throttle

current.name: runs test:unit as one direct Vitest command with no lane or ledger import
fixes the unit cap at three and lets VITEST_MAX_WORKERS lower it only

rationale: The unit stage no longer reserves any ledger budget. Its replacement semantics are the direct no-ledger command and the deterministic three-worker cap, which jointly replace fair-grant selection without reintroducing the old throttle.

baseName: reserveVitestLaneBudget REUSES a parent grant (preserves run-v2 ledger behavior)

current.name: runs test:unit as one direct Vitest command with no lane or ledger import

rationale: Parent-grant reuse is intentionally absent from the new unit path because test:unit bypasses lane and ledger orchestration entirely. The replacement pins that architectural boundary through the exact direct command and import audit.

multi-repo archived sessions expose one worktree row per repoWorktrees entry
scan skips archived worktree paths still referenced by a live session
